diagnosisProfile.description: Initial technical diagnosis profile aligned with the consolidated pilot benchmark campaigns and focused on a first evidence-based interpretation of the officially rerun results.

scenario.purpose: Pilot Worker Count Sweep - Single RPC Worker
scenario.notes: Worker-count-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. All non-worker-count dimensions are inherited from B0 and the active RPC worker set remains fully co-located on sc-app-02, exactly following the baseline placement mode. The only intended experimental delta is the active worker count.

scenario.purpose: Pilot Worker Count Sweep - Two RPC Workers
scenario.notes: Worker-count-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. This scenario reproduces the official two-worker baseline layout and keeps both active RPC workers fully co-located on sc-app-02. The only intended experimental delta within the Worker Count Sweep family is the active worker count.

scenario.purpose: Pilot Worker Count Sweep - Three RPC Workers
scenario.notes: Worker-count-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. Model, workload, prompt, server manifest, and placement mode are all inherited from B0; the third active RPC worker is added while preserving the same co-located placement on sc-app-02 so that only the active worker count varies.

unsupportedReports.reason: Scenario worker-count non supportato sotto i vincoli correnti della baseline fissata: il placement 'colocated_sc_app_02' deve restare invariato e, dopo l'auto-apply e il tempo di stabilizzazione previsto, almeno un pod RPC richiesto dallo scenario rimane non schedulabile sul cluster corrente per vincoli di risorse e/o affinity.

scenario.purpose: Pilot Workload Sweep - Smoke Single User
scenario.notes: Workload-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. This scenario defines the single-user smoke workload while inheriting the fixed model, worker scenario, placement scenario, prompt, temperature, and request timeout directly from the baseline. The only intended experimental delta within the Workload Sweep family is the resolved input workload profile.

scenario.purpose: Pilot Workload Sweep - Low Load Two Users
scenario.notes: Workload-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. This scenario defines the low-load two-user reference workload while inheriting the fixed model, worker scenario, placement scenario, prompt, temperature, and request timeout directly from the baseline. The only intended experimental delta within the Workload Sweep family is the resolved input workload profile.

scenario.purpose: Pilot Workload Sweep - Small Concurrency Four Users
scenario.notes: Workload-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. This scenario defines the small-concurrency four-user workload while inheriting the fixed model, worker scenario, placement scenario, prompt, temperature, and request timeout directly from the baseline. The only intended experimental delta within the Workload Sweep family is the resolved input workload profile.

scenario.purpose: Pilot Model Sweep - Baseline Llama 3.2 1B q4_k_m
scenario.notes: Model-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. This scenario reproduces the official baseline model and inherits the fixed worker scenario, placement scenario, workload scenario, prompt, temperature, and request timeout directly from the baseline. The only intended experimental delta within the Model Sweep family is the resolved model and its derived server manifest.

Model-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. This scenario changes the model family member to the 1B q8_0 variant while inheriting the fixed worker scenario, placement scenario, workload scenario, prompt, temperature, and request timeout directly from the baseline. The only intended experimental delta within the Model Sweep family is the resolved model and its derived server manifest.

Model-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. This scenario changes the model family member to the 3B q4_k_m variant while inheriting the fixed worker scenario, placement scenario, workload scenario, prompt, temperature, and request timeout directly from the baseline. The only intended experimental delta within the Model Sweep family is the resolved model and its derived server manifest.

scenario.purpose: Pilot Model Sweep - Llama 3.2 3B Instruct q8_0
scenario.notes: Model-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. This scenario changes the model family member to the 3B q8_0 variant while inheriting the fixed worker scenario, placement scenario, workload scenario, prompt, temperature, and request timeout directly from the baseline. The only intended experimental delta within the Model Sweep family is the resolved model and its derived server manifest.

scenario.purpose: Pilot Placement Sweep - Co-Located on sc-app-02
scenario.notes: Placement-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. This scenario reproduces the official baseline co-located placement on sc-app-02 while inheriting the fixed model, worker count, workload, prompt, temperature, and request timeout directly from B0. The only intended experimental delta within the Placement Sweep family is the resolved placement policy and its derived topology directory.

scenario.purpose: Pilot Placement Sweep - Distributed Two-Node Placement
scenario.notes: Placement-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. This scenario keeps the baseline model, worker count, workload, prompt, temperature, and request timeout fixed while switching only the worker topology to the distributed two-node layout. The only intended experimental delta within the Placement Sweep family is the resolved placement policy and its derived topology directory.

findings.title: La validazione minima end-to-end risulta disponibile come base di affidabilità funzionale.
findings.implication: La pipeline di benchmark parte da una base funzionale verificata e non da un setup puramente teorico.

findings.title: Il workload più intenso aumenta sensibilmente la latenza, segnalando possibile saturazione del sistema.
findings.implication: Il cluster appare più fragile quando cresce la concorrenza; la sola crescita del carico non si traduce necessariamente in aumento proporzionale del throughput utile.

findings.title: La dimensione del modello emerge come driver dominante della latenza media.
findings.implication: Nel cluster CPU-only attuale il passaggio a modelli più grandi sembra incidere molto più della variazione fine di topologia o workload leggero.

familyJudgments.title: Nel confronto iniziale tra configurazioni misurate il numero di worker non produce un miglioramento abbastanza marcato.
familyJudgments.implication: Il passaggio da W1 a W2 è stato osservato, ma il beneficio sulla latenza media resta sotto la soglia diagnostica configurata; eventuali scenari superiori unsupported limitano inoltre la valutazione oltre il perimetro misurato.

familyJudgments.title: La famiglia workload mostra un segnale netto di deterioramento prestazionale all'aumentare del carico.
familyJudgments.implication: Nel cluster corrente il workload è un driver rilevante della latenza e suggerisce una soglia di fragilità più evidente nelle configurazioni più spinte.

familyJudgments.title: La famiglia models mostra una penalità dimensionale netta e dominante sulla latenza media.
familyJudgments.implication: Nel cluster corrente la scelta del modello incide fortemente sul comportamento del servizio e rappresenta uno dei driver principali della performance osservata.

familyJudgments.title: Nel perimetro osservato la famiglia placement non mostra ancora un effetto abbastanza marcato da essere considerato dominante.
familyJudgments.implication: Le configurazioni di placement sono state confrontate rispetto alla baseline co-located PL1, ma la differenza di latenza resta sotto la soglia diagnostica configurata; nel cluster corrente il placement non emerge ancora come driver principale delle performance.
